definition: VP's CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance gradient is the sole enabling technique: it preserves inputs whose intercepted comparison operands move incrementally closer to the gate target, outperforming both naive (no gradient at all) and value_profile_cmplog (gradient plus I2S, where the added I2S stage regresses performance by diluting the corpus or consuming per-execution budget). Gate structures range from simple equality to switch-case dispatch to bitmask presence; the common thread is that the CMP_MAP bucket signal is both necessary and sufficient while I2S is actively counterproductive. These are MIXED-edge cases (vp>naive AND vp>vpc) requiring manual review to confirm whether the anti-I2S edge is a consistent VP_pro-with-I2S-overhead phenomenon rather than noise.

Plain naive havoc mutation (BytesDelete/ByteFlip/BytesRandSet/TokenReplace) is sufficient to produce the required codepoint-pair alignment; both I2S (cmplog) and context-sensitive coverage (naive_ctx) are actively counterproductive — I2S diverts mutation energy without providing a useful gradient for this simple equality, and ctx_coverage fragments the edge signal. The gate is a shallow paired equality (b==SIN_DOT AND a==SHIN) with no deep parse state, so no gradient technique provides lift and the overhead of introspection regresses both. This is a MIXED-edge singleton (naive>cmplog AND naive>naive_ctx) requiring manual review: it is anti-I2S and anti-ctx_coverage simultaneously, bridging two separate anti- families, and may warrant its own template or merging with an I2S_anti cluster.

Havoc BytesDeleteMutator shrinks the input below a fixed minimum read length (e.g., a header size constant), causing a record-count read to return fewer than expected and firing the failure branch; I2S and context-coverage techniques keep their corpora anchored to large, structurally valid inputs and never emit inputs short enough to trigger the underflow.
